svg.title: Slipway Run storage and mutation ordering
svg.desc: Run data lives under the repository Git common directory, not under a worktree literal .git path. Each Run directory holds an append-only journal, a replaceable projection, a validated lock artifact, and content-addressed materials. A mutation writes referenced material before any journal event may point to it, synchronizes the journal, and only then replaces the projection. If projection refresh fails after a committed journal write, Slipway reports the committed mutation and the stale projection instead of claiming a rollback. Writer serialization uses an OS directory lock on Unix and a named mutex on Windows.
svg.metadata:
flowchart TB subgraph layout["<git-common-dir>/slipway/runs/<run-id>/"] journal["journal.jsonl · append-only transition record · the recovery authority"] projection["run.json · replaceable projection · rebuildable from the journal"] lock["run.lock · validated coordination artifact · not the sole writer guard"] materials["materials/ · accepted issue sections by content digest"] end
Start([mutation requested]) --> Identity[revalidate workspace identity
root · per-worktree git dir · git common dir] Identity --> Acquire[acquire the writer guard
Unix: OS lock on the opened Run dir
Windows: named mutex] Acquire --> Material[1 · write referenced material first
no journal event may point to absent bytes] Material --> Append[2 · append the journal event] Append --> Sync[3 · synchronize the journal
Unix: file + directory fsync where supported
Windows: file flush only] Sync --> Commit{committed} Commit --> Project[4 · replace the projection] Project --> Done([mutation complete]) Project -->|refresh fails| Report[report committed mutation + stale projection
never claim a rollback]
svg.text: Slipway · Run storage and mutation ordering
the journal is the recovery authority · the projection is disposable · material exists before anything points to it
ON DISK
<git-common-dir>/slipway/runs/<run-id>/
the Git common directory — not necessarily a worktree's literal .git path
journal.jsonl
append-only transition record · order is the recovery record
run.json
replaceable projection · may be rebuilt from the journal
materials/
accepted issue sections addressed by content digest
run.lock
validated coordination artifact · not the sole writer guard
ORDER OF ONE MUTATION
revalidate workspace identity · acquire the writer guard (Unix: OS dir lock · Windows: named mutex)
Write referenced material first
no journal event may ever point at bytes that do not exist yet
Append the journal event
one writer at a time, enforced by the platform lock implementation
Synchronize the journal — the commit boundary
Unix: file + directory fsync where supported · Windows: file flush only; `doctor` reports the level
Replace the projection
only after the journal is durable — never before
if projection refresh fails
Committed journal write, stale projection
Slipway reports the committed mutation and the stale projection.
It does not claim a rollback that did not happen.
IDENTITY IS RECHECKED EVERY TIME
Every load and mutation rediscovers the canonical worktree root,
per-worktree Git directory, and Git common directory, and compares
them before touching a journal. Reusing a path for another worktree
or retargeting Git metadata fails before any byte is modified.
WHAT INSPECTION MAY NOT DO
`status` is filesystem-read-only: it creates no namespace or lock,
changes no permissions, and repairs no journal bytes. Unreadable
local Runs stay visible as `unavailable_runs` rather than hidden.
Never hand-edit `journal.jsonl` or `run.json`.
